messages.content: Write a blog post where Braehead Centre retired its Brae-lien mascots in May 2018 as part of the Intu Family Club launch, The 3 aliens, Ben, Bailey and Belle, vanished without a trace,

Title: Saying Goodbye to the Beloved Brae-liens: Braehead Centre Ushers in a New Era with Intu Family Club

As regular patrons of the Braehead Centre in Glasgow know, it has been a place of not just shopping, but of community and family entertainment. For years, visitors have been charmed by the interstellar antics of three special residents: Ben, Bailey, and Belle — the Brae-lien mascots. With their otherworldly appeal and friendly presence, these extraterrestrial ambassadors created an atmosphere of fun and adventure for families venturing to the centre. However, as all good things come to an end, May 2018 marked the closing of a chapter for Braehead, as the Centre made the decision to bid farewell to its playful aliens.

The trio became an iconic part of Braehead Centre's identity since their arrival and have seen generations of children grow. They've been a staple in seasonal events, made surprise appearances, and always provided a photogenic opportunity for those eager to capture memories of their shopping experience. It was impossible to meander through the mall without spotting a youngster tugging at a parent's hand, eager to greet and high-five their favorite Brae-lien.

The reason for their departure? Change is afoot at Braehead Centre, with the launch of the Intu Family Club. In an effort to keep up with the evolving landscape of retail and entertainment, Intu (the company behind Braehead) decided it was time to freshen things up. With the new Family Club, the centre hoped to offer a broad range of activities, workshops and events designed to engage and delight every member of the family, strengthening family ties through the power of shared experiences.

While the sudden disappearance of Ben, Bailey, and Belle stirred a mix of emotions among the Centre’s loyal visitors — surprise, nostalgia, and curiosity about what's to come — the management assured that the future was bright and full of new opportunities. The pillars of the Intu Family Club promised to be interactive, inclusive, and innovative, building on the legacy that the Brae-liens helped to establish.

Though no formal farewell party was held, the outpouring of memories and tributes from shoppers on social media served as a fitting tribute to the cherished mascots who had become more like family. These digital anecdotes showcased the profound impact the Brae-liens had on the flavour and life of Braehead Centre.

We learned an important lesson from our cosmic friends’ departure: change, while at times bittersweet, is an opportunity for growth and new experiences. As families continue to visit Braehead Centre, they'll discover new characters, events, and a reinvigorated space dedicated to creating moments just as memorable as those provided by Ben, Bailey, and Belle.

The Centre invites all who miss the Brae-liens to look forward with anticipation. It encourages patrons, both loyal and new, to become a part of the Intu Family Club, where the focus remains on delivering joy and laughter, albeit without our otherworldly companions.

As they vanish without a trace into the annals of Braehead history, the Brae-lien mascots leave behind more than just their space boots and playful antics. They leave a legacy of happiness, connection, and the reminder that sometimes, the joy we find in the little things is truly out of this world.

Write a forum conversation where their kid holds a "funeral" for them on ROBLOX, and even makes a farewell animation in GoAnimate, However, over the next few weeks, Dick and Dom, as well as Jamie and Anna will be appearing to entertain kids, likely to cheer them up, This is being dubbed "Da Bungalow VS Toonattik"

**Thread Title**: My Kid Held a "Funeral" for Braehead's Brae-liens in Roblox!

**ForumUser1**: Just when you think you've seen it all... My kid loved those Brae-lien mascots from the Braehead Centre so much that when they retired, they threw a "funeral" for them on Roblox complete with a little digital grave and a GoAnimate farewell animation. 😅 Anyone else's kids going to extreme lengths to bid adieu?

**ForumUser2**: That’s adorable and sad at the same time! 😂 My son just constantly asks when they’re coming back. He doesn’t quite grasp the concept of "retirement," I guess.

**ForumUser3**: Aww, that's sweet and super creative. My daughter used to hug them every single time we went shopping. I didn't know how to break the news to her, so I just showed her the animation your kid made. It actually helped, so tell them thanks!

**ForumUser1**: Will do, @ForumUser3! I was impressed by their Roblox skills, not gonna lie. How is your daughter handling it now?

**ForumUser4**: That’s proper dedication. My own kids found the news tough too. But have you heard Dick and Dom are coming in to cheer up the young ones? 😁

**ForumUser5**: Yep, it’s apparently called "Da Bungalow VS Toonattik." I used to watch those shows! It sounds like an epic showdown but with fun. I think it should really help lift the kids' spirits after losing the Brae-liens.

**ForumUser1**: Seriously? Dick and Dom are legends! And Jamie and Anna too? I might be more excited than my kid. 😆 When are they supposed to be showing up?

**ForumUser5**: The Centre’s website says over the next few weeks. They’ve got a schedule up for different events. Should be a blast!

**ForumUser6**: My kids are obsessed with Roblox; how do I find this 'funeral' they might get a kick out of it, or should I say... it might give them some closure?

**ForumUser1**: It's in a private server, but I can ask if my kid doesn’t mind sharing it. They really did put a lot of work into that farewell animation. Nearly tore up watching it.

**ForumUser7**: It’s amazing what these kids can do with technology these days. I'll be sure to take mine to see Dick and Dom, and Jamie and Anna. It’s nice to see the centre putting in the effort to transition with some familiar faces.

**ForumUser8**: Does anyone feel like this is more for the parents though? I swear I'm going to be at the front row, fanboying over Dick and Dom... My kids don't even know them. 🤣

**ForumUser2**: We'll be there too! Hopefully, this means the start of a new tradition for the kids - one that maybe they'll throw Roblox parties for in the future! 😄

**ForumUser9**: Let’s not forget to show some appreciation to the Braehead staff for this. It's not easy to shift gears like that, and they're doing a lot to keep the magic alive for our kids (and us).

**ForumUser10**: Totally agree, @ForumUser9. And @ForumUser1, your kid has set the bar high, I'm expecting great things from my child's next Roblox creation now. Peace out, Brae-liens! 🚀

**ForumUser1**: Thanks all, for the support! It feels like we're all part of a Braehead family in here. Here's to new memories and plenty of laughs at "Da Bungalow VS Toonattik" events! 💖👽🌌

(End of Thread)
Write a follow up soon after this where the KikoRiki characters appear in costumed form, this is still part of the "mourning" period,

**Thread Title**: KikoRiki Characters Join Braehead's Mascot "Mourning" Period

**ForumUser1**: Guys, update on the Braehead Centre's mascots situation! After the Brae-liens left, who would've thought we'd have KikoRiki characters show up in full costume? My kid is absolutely over the moon seeing Krash and Chiko waddling around the shopping centre!

**ForumUser2**: KikoRiki!? No way! My little ones love that show. It's like a whole mascot revival over there. I've never seen them in costume form before, though. Are they part of the official events?

**ForumUser3**: Just saw them today! They were handing out balloons, and it definitely lifted the spirits of not just the kids. Seeing those colorful characters dancing around is pretty heartwarming.

**ForumUser4**: Wow, that’s a surprise! I remember my nephew watching their shows on repeat. I didn't think they were that popular here, but it’s a clever way to help the kids "move on" from the Brae-liens.

**ForumUser5**: They're sooo cute! But not gonna lie, it's pretty bittersweet. The kiddos were jumping with joy, yet my eldest whispered, "I miss the Brae-liens" while hugging one of the KikoRiki characters.

**ForumUser6**: My little one didn’t notice the Brae-liens missing until the KikoRiki gang showed up... kind of a backfired distraction for us, but hey, overall it seems to be helping most families.

**ForumUser1**: @ForumUser2, yeah they're part of the ongoing "mourning" activities. I think they're trying to follow up on the hype of Dick and Dom, and Jamie and Anna's appearances. It’s a good move.

**ForumUser7**: This "mourning" period is turning out to be a full-blown mascot extravaganza. 😂 Anyone know how long they’ll be around?

**ForumUser8**: The Centre's website says the KikoRiki characters will be here for the next month with varying appearances. I'm taking my kids this weekend and they are psyched!

**ForumUser9**: I wonder what's next after the KikoRiki visit? It's like there's a farewell tour for the Brae-liens. These transitions are turning into full-on events!

**ForumUser10**: My guess? They've got a big reveal planned. Maybe new permanent mascots? Seems like they’re pulling out all the stops to both honour the past mascots and generate excitement for whatever's next.

**ForumUser11**: Kinda smart when you think about it. They get us talking about the Brae-liens, bring in nostalgic acts for the parents, and now KikoRiki for the kids... they're creating a lot of buzz.

**ForumUser12**: If anyone's heading there, could you snap some pictures? We're out of town, and my kids would flip to see the KikoRiki crew hanging out at Braehead!

**ForumUser1**: Sure thing, @ForumUser12! I'll be your Braehead correspondent. 😄 Got to admit, watching all the families smile with these characters is making the visits feel special again, even if I do miss our alien friends.

(End of Thread)
Write a short tweet a year later where the Family Club has a wordsearch, and one word is in the jumbled words but not listed

Just spotted a little #EasterEgg in the @intuBraehead Family Club wordsearch! "Brae-liens" is a hidden gem in the puzzle but not on the list. A sweet nod to our beloved space friends from the past. 🛸💙 #RememberingBraeLiens #BraeheadFun #WordSearchSurprise🔍
Write a short tweet a few years later where the centre makes MASSIVE changes, Shops closing everywhere, but one good change

Major transformations at @intuBraehead with a wave of shop closures hitting hard. 😢 But it's not all gloom! Excited to see the new state-of-the-art cinema complex ushering in a fresh era of entertainment. 🎬🍿 Positive vibes! #BraeheadRevamp #CinemaLoversRejoice #ChangeIsGood
Write a follow up tweet where a familiar plush is spotted in the rain, Also, the ice rink is CLOSING FOR 6 WEEKS, The poster calls this "OUTRAGEOUS"

Spotted in the rain: a lonely Brae-lien plush outside @intuBraehead, tugging at the heartstrings! 😭 Meanwhile, the iconic ice rink is closing for 6 WEEKS for refurbishment – OUTRAGEOUS timing as we head into holidays. Skaters and families are not impressed. ⛸️😡 #BraeheadBlues #IceRinkClosure

Write a follow up where the centre has a Halloween event with Scooby Doo, While embarking on this quest we spot 3 grave stones, Shaggy distracts us just seconds after looking at them saying "The bad guy's getting away"

Creepy capers at @intuBraehead's Halloween event with Scooby-Doo leading the charge! 🎃🕵️ But, what’s this? Three mysterious gravestones spotted on the quest – could it be a tribute to our beloved Brae-liens?? Shaggy's timely call to chase the 'bad guy' left us in suspense! 🏃‍♂️💨 #BraeheadMystery #ScoobyDooAdventure
Write a forum convo where a user takes a look, and it even has then etched on it, They got to look at it for 8 seconds before Shaggy interrupted it

**Thread Title**: Found Brae-lien Tribute at Halloween Event before Shaggy's Interrupting Interruption!

**MysteryMallGoer**: Just had to post about this! At the @intuBraehead Halloween event, we were with Scooby-Doo and crew on their spooky adventure when I stumbled upon three gravestones. And guess what? They had the Brae-liens etched on them! Only got a solid 8-second look before Shaggy herded us away. 😲👽 #BraeheadHalloween #MysterySolved

**SpookyShopper**: No way, you actually saw that? That's an amazing find! 😍 Did anyone else get to see them?

**AlienAdmirer**: I caught a glimpse too! Managed a quick pic before we were rushed off by Shaggy. It felt like such a heartfelt homage to Ben, Bailey, and Belle. 🖤

**GlasgowGhostHunter**: They're really keeping the spirit of those aliens alive, aren't they? Such a clever touch to include that in the Halloween festivities. 

**MallMemories**: It’s bittersweet, those little nods to past favorites. Makes me miss the days when the Brae-liens roamed the mall. 

**ScoobySnackLover**: 8 seconds, huh? That sounds like a new record for the Halloween event. Shaggy’s been on top form, rushing people along from every 'clue'! 😆

**InterstellarInquiry**: Is it just me, or does it seem like they want us to remember the Brae-liens but not linger on them? Feels a bit rushed, even for a Halloween event.

**ThrillerThrifty**: It's marketing gold is what it is! They know how to tug those nostalgic heartstrings and get people talking. Clever, Braehead, very clever!

**WeMissTheAliens**: Anyone planning to go back and get a better look? I’d love to try and read what’s on those gravestones properly.

**CosmicCruiser**: I'm tempted, @WeMissTheAliens! Was too wrapped up in the mystery hunt to fully appreciate the nod to our extraterrestrial friends. Any volunteers for a return excursion?

**MotherOfMartians**: Count me in! Those Brae-liens were like family to my kids. I think a proper look is in order... for posterity, you know? 😢

**GhoulGlasgow**: Let's organize a little trip back! But this time, we’ll make sure Shaggy doesn't cut our sleuthing short!

**MysteryMallGoer**: Love this community! Going back for a better look could be the mini adventure we all need. Plus, it's another chance to see Scooby-Doo!

**BraeheadBuzz**: You guys are awesome. Honoring the Brae-liens while having fun – that's what it's all about. Keep us updated on your findings!

(End of Thread)

Write a follow up where a kid asks about it and the villan, who is about to be unmasked says "THEYVE BEEN DEAD FOR YEARS" When he is unmasked he says the iconic "And I would have gotten away with it if it weren't for you meddling kids"

**Thread Title**: Villain's Dramatic Reveal at #BraeheadHalloween!

**MeddlingMum**: Epic moment at the @intuBraehead Halloween event today! My kiddo asked the 'villain' about the mysterious Brae-lien gravestones, and just before being unmasked he exclaimed, "THEY'VE BEEN DEAD FOR YEARS!" So dramatic! And of course, the classic line followed post-unmasking... 😄👻 #MysteryIncMoment

**ScoobyDooFan**: Haha, that's brilliant! The actors they hired really take their roles seriously. Extra points for maintaining character right to the end! 

**SpookySleuthKid**: I was there for that! The whole crowd gasped when he said it. Gotta say, this year's event has really been top-notch with its surprises.

**VelmaVision**: I just love how they're weaving the history of Braehead into the event narrative. Also, can’t get enough of the "meddling kids" trope!

**BooCrewDad**: Who was behind the mask? Anyone you recognized? The suspense is killing me here!

**FrightNightQueen**: I heard it was one of the local theater actors who's known for his villainous portrayals. Bet he enjoyed throwing out that iconic Scooby-Doo line!

**MeddlingMum**: @BooCrewDad, it was just a hired actor, but he certainly had that classic villain vibe down to a tee. My child was both thrilled and spooked!

**EerieExplorer**: Did anyone manage to get a video of this? I'm gutted I missed it – classic Scooby-Doo reveals are the best!

**GhostHunterGaz**: Did they give any more info on what the meaning behind the gravestones was? Or just the dramatic line and nothing more?

**MeddlingMum**: @GhostHunterGaz, no more info, I'm afraid! Just the dramatics for the unmasking. It's like a teaser for something more, or maybe just a bit of fun?

**ZombieZoomer**: It's these cute and quirky moments that my kids will remember forever! Braehead’s really outdone themselves with this Halloween extravaganza. 

**CandlelitCreeper**: I'm tearing up a bit thinking about the Brae-liens tbh... That line felt like such a nod to all the long-time shoppers and their fond memories. Well played!

**DaphneDare**: These kind of events are what community memories are made of. I hope the Centre keeps up the good work in the years to come!

**MeddlingMum**: Agreed, @DaphneDare! It's a bittersweet, but fun way to honor past traditions while creating new ones. And to the villain actor – you stole the show! 🎭

(End of Thread)
Write a follow up the next Summer, where a cool magic adventure begins, And what it leads to is amazing, In other news H&M is CLOSING

**Thread Title**: Summer Magic Adventure Unfolds at @intuBraehead – H&M Closure Update

**MagicMallAdventurer**: Just when you think @intuBraehead has shown us all its tricks – this summer they’ve rolled out a MAGICAL adventure! 🧙‍♂️✨ Kids got to embark on a quest with wizards, solve riddles, and it all led to an incredible new indoor playground area! Jaw-dropping fun for the little ones! 

**FashionForwardMom**: Can't believe H&M is closing though...😢 It’s always been my go-to for quick fashion fixes! But wow, the magic adventure sounds like a fantastic addition for summer!

**WizardryWonder**: That indoor playground is like nothing I've ever seen! My kids are already begging me to take them back. Definitely softens the blow of losing a shopping staple. 🛍️➡️🏰

**SpellboundShopper**: Does anyone know if the magic adventure is replacing H&M or is it located elsewhere in the centre?

**MagicMallAdventurer**: @SpellboundShopper It's set up in a different area, not where H&M was. It's like a secret garden inside Braehead – totally separate!

**RetailRaven**: Shame about H&M. 🖤 But Braehead just knows how to keep the place lively. A magic quest? That's thinking out of the box (or a magician’s hat)! 🎩 

**EnchantedParent**: The quest led to the kids being 'knighted' by a wizard! 🗡️ My little one hasn’t stopped talking about it. Loving Braehead’s way of creating unique experiences.

**PlayfulParent**: Big props to @intuBraehead for arranging something so interactive. It beats just another static play area. Has anyone heard if they’re doing similar events?

**RetailRaven**: Heard through the grapevine that there might be more themed adventures lined up for each season. If summer is this good, can't wait to see what autumn brings!

**HighStHearts**: Super gutted about H&M, it's the end of an era. 😔 Let's hope the magical adventures can conjure up some new shops too! 🛍️✨

**MagicMallAdventurer**: @PlayfulParent, you bet! Rumor says autumn might be enchanted forest themed?! Kids could do with more of this, especially with less and less physical shops to visit.

**EnchantedParent**: My kids are definitely getting a summer to remember, thanks to Braehead! This new direction with experiences over stores could be the potion for success.

**PlaygroundPundit**: Here's a thought – maybe @intuBraehead is becoming less about shopping and more about interactive experiences? Could be a new trend... 🤔

**HighStHearts**: @PlaygroundPundit That seems to be the way forward for a lot of places now. Retail has changed, and it's all about the EXPERIENCE!

**MagicMallAdventurer**: Whatever the future holds, it's shaping up to be one magical ride at the Centre. Great to see kids and families having so much fun again! 🌟👪

(End of Thread)
